title: PILOT TESTING DIGITAL STORIES TO REDUCE COVID-19 VACCINE HESITANCY AMONG HISPANIC PARENTS
keywords: phase i story creation (n=10) + cab selection (top 4)

summary.hook: This poster describes a two-phase project using digital storytelling (DST) to address COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy among Hispanic parents, guided by the Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB).
summary.summary_30s: This poster describes a two-phase project using digital storytelling (DST) to address COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy among Hispanic parents, guided by the Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB). In Phase I, 10 formerly hesitant parents created digital stories and a community advisory board selected the top 4 for use. In Phase II, 80 Hispanic parents were randomized to DST vs control videos, with surveys at baseline, immediate post-test, and 2-month follow-up; results suggest DST increased vaccine intention more than control (Δ +1.2 vs +0.6) and parents who viewed DSTs were more likely to vaccinate their children (reported OR=5.20, RR=3.10) and at follow-up 50% vs 16% vaccinated (DST vs control).
talktrack_2m: Focus: Explore feasibility, acceptability, and preliminary trends of using DSTs to address COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy among Hispanic parents (guided by TPB).

Approach: Population: Hispanic parents; Phase I includes formerly hesitant parents; Phase II includes 80 Hispanic parents randomized. Primary outcome: Vaccine intention change; child vaccination at follow-up; TPB construct changes.
Finding: Background claim: Hispanic children are less likely to initiate/complete COVID-19 vaccine series, largely due to parental hesitancy.

Why it matters: Authors’ stated conclusions: DST is feasible and acceptable and shows promise as a low-cost, scalable, culturally-tailored approach to address COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy among Hispanic parents; potentially applicable to other vaccines and health resources. Implications (poster-grounded): Narrative DST interventions may outperform standard health messages in persuasiveness for this population, as supported by focus group quotes and higher follow-up vaccination in the DST arm. Community co-creation (CHWs, CAB story selection) is integral to feasibility and cultural tailoring. Mechanism/explanation (ONLY if stated): Guided by TPB; DSTs produced emotional resonance and increased believability, which may influence TPB constructs (details not specified). Connections to prior work (ONLY if referenced): References to disparities literature and “past research explored DST to increase other vaccine uptake (HPV vaccine).” Two references listed: Anglin et al.
key_numbers: * Phase I: 10 formerly hesitant Hispanic parents created DSTs.
* Surveys: baseline, immediately post-intervention, and 2-month follow-up.
Hispanic parents; Phase I includes formerly hesitant parents; Phase II includes 80 Hispanic parents randomized.
Phase I: 10 formerly hesitant parents created DSTs; CAB selected top 4 stories.
Phase II: 80 Hispanic parents randomized (DST vs control).
takeaways: Background claim: Hispanic children are less likely to initiate/complete COVID-19 vaccine series, largely due to parental hesitancy.

Authors’ stated conclusions: DST is feasible and acceptable and shows promise as a low-cost, scalable, culturally-tailored approach to address COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy among Hispanic parents; potentially applicable to other vaccines and health resources.
limitations: The poster does not list formal study limitations.
The poster leaves open this clarification: What were the exact vaccine intention and TPB construct scales (items, range), and how were Δ scores computed?

what_to_ask.question: The 2-month vaccination rates were 50% (DST) vs 16% (control)—what were the raw counts behind those percentages?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This gets the presenter to interpret the main result instead of repeating the title.

what_to_ask.question: How did you handle participants who didn’t watch all assigned videos (dose/exposure measurement)?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This probes confidence, assumptions, or limitations without sounding hostile.

what_to_ask.question: What would it take to scale DST delivery through clinics, schools, or community organizations?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This opens a useful conversation about why the work matters or where it goes next.
